fallback.reason: No local cangjie skill was available in this runtime's skill list, so the entry was distilled using the available book2skill guidance and local skill format conventions.

metadataSupplemented.reason: The source content file was only a minimal BPMN.org supplement and did not contain the representative book content or enough BPM method detail.
items: Used local metadata.json to identify the entry as the book 'Fundamentals of Business Process Management' by Marlon Dumas et al.
Used the local Springer link from metadata.json as the representative book locator.
Used the local BPMN.org supplement as official BPMN/OMG context for BPMN 2.0 notation.
Supplemented the operational BPM lifecycle from the local metadata note: process identification, modeling, analysis, redesign, automation, monitoring, and optimization.

closureReview.scope: Closed-loop review of SKILL.md, test-prompts.json, and audit.json.

qualityChecks.standaloneKnowledge: SKILL.md provides BPM lifecycle, modeling, execution, monitoring, redesign, recovery, and boundary guidance without requiring source files.
qualityChecks.runtimeDependencyPolicy: External files, webpages, original books, source reports, external source folder, distilled source material, and out-of-directory material are provenance only and not runtime dependencies.
qualityChecks.triggerContract: Trigger, non-trigger, activation gate, and execution gate are explicit.
qualityChecks.workflowAndDeliverables: Workflow, output artifacts, failure/recovery/idempotency, and hard rules are present.
qualityChecks.testPromptCoverage: test-prompts.json includes should_trigger, should_not_trigger, edge_cases, and compliance_expectations.

remainingRisks: Original source snapshot was incomplete, but all runtime BPM guidance required for this skill is compressed into SKILL.md.

runtimeDependencyPolicy: Closed skill: execute from SKILL.md; use references/source-notes.md only as local optional provenance; do not require external source folders, webpages, original books, source reports, machine-local paths, or cross-skill files.
